text: Wrist Articulation II is a much more difficult and practical version of the wrist articulation exercises. It presents you a whole entire visual field of spheres surrounded by glass globes, which have openings at specific angles. The goal is to touch the sphere inside the glass globe with the designated instrument without touching the sides of the globe or applying pressure to the glass.

Camera utilization is important in this exercise as to provide the appropriate view of the opening in the glass globe and thus allows you to minimize penalties in regards to touching the globe or applying unnecessary pressure. As mentioned in the wrist articulation one video, by using the hand controls to straighten out the instrument tips as you enter through the glass opening, this allows you to touch the inner sphere while the remainder of the instrument avoids contact with the glass edges or the external pressure on the globe. Take your time as you pass your instrument through the glass opening to maximize your accuracy. And avoid hitting the glass spheres as you move your instruments around the field. Success in this exercise requires good camera coordination, two-arm coordination, and maximizing the degrees of freedom with wrist articulation.
